title: WOS Integration Profile Document
description: A WOS Integration Profile Document per the WOS Integration Profile specification v1.0. A parallel seam document that declares named integration bindings for a WOS workflow: Arazzo sequences for multi-step API orchestration, CWL-informed tool descriptors for non-HTTP invocations, CloudEvents extension attributes for event interoperability, external policy engine bridges (XACML, OPA, Cedar), and standard request-response/event/callback patterns. Each binding declares interface references, input/output mappings, retry policies, and optional Formspec Definition contracts for request and response validation. The Integration Profile adds structure to the kernel's invokeService action (Kernel S9.2) without introducing new kernel seams. WOS MUST NOT alter core Formspec processing semantics.

^x-.$comment: Vendor extensions per Kernel spec §10.6 (extensions escape hatch)

$wosIntegrationProfile.description: WOS Integration Profile specification version. MUST be '1.0'. Identifies this document as a WOS Integration Profile Document and pins the specification version. Processors reject documents with unsupported versions.

x-lm.intent: Document type marker and version pin for WOS Integration Profile documents

targetWorkflow.description: The WOS Kernel Document this profile targets. Specifies URL and compatible version range.

x-lm.intent: Binds this integration profile to a specific kernel workflow and version range

version.description: Version of this Integration Profile Document. SemVer is RECOMMENDED.

title.description: Human-readable name for this integration profile. Displayed in tooling and provenance when tracing which profile resolved an invokeService binding.

description.description: Human-readable description of the integration profile's purpose and scope. Documents which external systems are connected and which integration patterns are used.
examples: Declares Arazzo sequences for eligibility checks, request-response bindings for income verification, and an OPA policy engine bridge for entitlement decisions.

bindings.description: Named integration bindings. Keys are binding identifiers referenced by invokeService serviceRef (Kernel S9.2). Values are typed binding declarations.

x-lm.intent: The map of named integration bindings that the workflow can invoke

url.description: Canonical URI of the target WOS Kernel Document. The integration processor resolves service bindings from this profile when the kernel document's URI matches this value at invocation time.

compatibleVersions.description: SemVer range expression for compatible kernel document versions.

IntegrationBinding.description: A named integration binding declaration. The type property determines which additional properties are valid.

type.description: Integration binding type (Integration Profile S3.2). Standard types: request-response, event-emit, event-consume, callback, arazzo-sequence, tool, policy-engine. Custom types use x- prefix.

x-lm.intent: Determines the kind of external system interaction this binding performs

description.description: Human-readable description of this integration binding and the external system it connects to. Recorded in provenance when the binding is invoked.
examples: Calls the federal income verification REST API and maps the verified income to the case file.

interface.description: OpenAPI interface reference. Required for request-response and callback bindings.

operation.description: Operation ID within the referenced OpenAPI document. Required for request-response and callback bindings. Must match the operationId in the referenced OpenAPI spec.

arazzoRef.description: URI reference to the Arazzo document that defines the multi-step API sequence. Required for arazzo-sequence bindings. The processor invokes the referenced Arazzo workflow and maps outputs back to case state via outputBinding (Integration Profile S3.4).

x-lm.intent: References the Arazzo workflow document for multi-step API orchestration

invocation.description: Invocation descriptor for non-HTTP tool bindings. Required for tool bindings.

engineType.description: Policy engine type (Integration Profile S8.2). Determines the request/response wire format and evaluation semantics. Standard types: 'xacml' (OASIS XACML 3.0), 'opa' (Open Policy Agent), 'cedar' (AWS Cedar). Custom types MUST use x- prefix.

x-lm.intent: Identifies the external policy engine technology

endpoint.description: The policy engine's decision endpoint. Required for policy-engine bindings.

additionalProperties.description: FEL expression or case state path whose evaluated value is passed as the engine parameter named by this map key.

contextMapping.description: Maps WOS evaluation context fields to policy engine request parameters. Keys are engine-specific parameter names; values are FEL expressions or case state paths. Required for policy-engine bindings.

decisionMapping.description: Maps the policy engine's response to a WOS governance decision. Required for policy-engine bindings.

eventType.description: CloudEvents type attribute value. Required for event-emit, event-consume, and callback (as callbackEventType) bindings.

callbackEventType.description: CloudEvents type attribute value of the expected callback event. Required for callback bindings. The processor listens for events with this type and routes them using the correlation rules.

correlation.description: Correlation rules for matching inbound events to workflow instances (Integration Profile S6). Required for event-consume and callback bindings.

additionalProperties.description: FEL expression or case state path that produces the value for the event data field named by this map key.

dataMapping.description: Maps case state paths to event data fields. Keys are event data field names; values are FEL expressions or case state paths. Used with event-emit bindings.

channel.description: Delivery channel hint (e.g., 'email', 'webhook', 'queue'). Implementation-defined. Used with event-emit bindings.

additionalProperties.description: FEL expression or case state path that provides the value for the service/tool input parameter named by this map key.

inputMapping.description: Maps case state paths to service/tool input parameters. Keys are parameter names; values are FEL expressions or case state paths.

additionalProperties.description: JSON Path expression into the service/tool response whose value is written to the case state path named by this map key.

outputBinding.description: Maps service/tool output to case state paths. Keys are case state paths; values are JSON Path expressions into the response.

requestContract.description: Formspec Definition contract for request validation (Integration Profile S4).

responseContract.description: Formspec Definition contract for response validation (Integration Profile S4).

resourceRequirements.description: Resource constraints for tool bindings (Integration Profile S3.6.2).

retry.description: Retry policy for failed invocations (Integration Profile S3.8).

timeout.description: Maximum time to wait for a response before the invocation is considered failed. ISO 8601 duration. The processor MUST record a timeout failure in provenance when exceeded.

idempotencyKeyExpression.description: FEL expression evaluated against case state to produce an idempotency key (Integration Profile S7). Maps to Kernel S9.3.

$ref.description: URI reference to the OpenAPI document describing the external service interface. The processor uses this document to construct requests and validate responses.

InvocationDescriptor.description: Non-HTTP invocation descriptor informed by CWL's CommandLineTool pattern (Integration Profile S3.6.1).

method.description: Invocation method for non-HTTP tool bindings (Integration Profile S3.6.1). Standard methods: 'command-line' (OS command), 'batch-file' (batch script), 'database-procedure' (stored procedure), 'graph-query' (SPARQL/Cypher). Custom methods MUST use x- prefix.

command.description: The command, stored procedure name, or query string to execute. Interpreted according to the 'method' field. FEL placeholders are not supported in command itself — use 'arguments' for dynamic values.

items.description: A single command argument string. FEL expressions enclosed in {{ }} delimiters are evaluated against case state before invocation and substituted inline.

arguments.description: Ordered command arguments. FEL expressions in {{ }} delimiters are evaluated against case state before invocation.

environment.description: Execution environment metadata (e.g., container image, runtime version). Implementation-defined.

ContractRef.description: Reference to a Formspec Definition used as a validation contract (Integration Profile S4).

definitionRef.description: URI reference to the Formspec Definition used for validation. The processor MUST delegate validation to a Formspec-conformant processor (Core S1.4).

x-lm.intent: References the Formspec Definition contract for structural validation

CorrelationRule.description: A correlation rule for matching inbound events to workflow instances (Integration Profile S6).

attribute.description: The CloudEvents attribute or extension attribute containing the correlation value.

caseStateMapping.description: The case state path whose value MUST match the correlation attribute.

DecisionMapping.description: Maps a policy engine's response to a WOS governance decision (Integration Profile S8.3).

permitPath.description: JSON Path expression into the policy engine's response document that evaluates to a boolean. When the resolved value is truthy, the decision is 'permit'. The processor maps this to a WOS governance decision (Integration Profile S8.3).

x-lm.intent: Extracts the permit/deny decision from the policy engine response

denyPath.description: JSON Path expression for explicit 'deny' in the engine response. If omitted, deny is inferred as the negation of the permitPath value. Use when the engine returns separate permit and deny fields.

reasonPath.description: JSON Path expression for the engine's human-readable reason string. Recorded in provenance to support audit and explanation requirements.

obligationsPath.description: JSON Path expression for obligations attached to the decision (XACML obligation pattern). Obligations are recorded in provenance and may be mapped to governance actions.

indeterminatePath.description: JSON Path expression for 'indeterminate' outcome (engine could not decide). When present and truthy, the decision is indeterminate. If omitted, results are always permit or deny with no indeterminate state.

RetryPolicy.description: Retry policy for failed integration invocations (Integration Profile S3.8).

maxAttempts.description: Maximum number of invocation attempts (including the initial attempt).

backoff.description: Backoff strategy between retries. 'fixed': constant interval. 'linear': interval grows linearly. 'exponential': interval doubles each retry.

initialInterval.description: Initial wait interval between the first and second invocation attempt. ISO 8601 duration. For exponential backoff, subsequent intervals double from this base.

maxInterval.description: Maximum interval between retries (for exponential/linear backoff). ISO 8601 duration.

ResourceRequirements.description: Resource constraints for tool invocations (Integration Profile S3.6.2).

maxExecutionTime.description: Maximum execution time. ISO 8601 duration. The processor MUST terminate the invocation if exceeded.

maxCores.description: Maximum CPU cores to allocate for this tool invocation. Enforcement is implementation-defined. Processors SHOULD log a warning when the limit is exceeded.

CloudEventsExtension.description: WOS CloudEvents extension attributes (Integration Profile S5). All events produced by a WOS workflow MUST include these attributes.

wosinstanceid.description: The unique URI identifier of the running workflow instance. Used by consuming systems to route events to the correct workflow instance and to correlate inbound callback events. MUST be globally unique across all tenants (Integration Profile S5.1).

x-lm.intent: Unique identifier of the running workflow instance for event routing

wosdefid.description: The workflow definition identifier. The canonical URL of the WOS Kernel Document that governs this workflow instance. Used by event consumers to dispatch events to the correct processor.

wosdefversion.description: The version of the WOS Kernel Document governing this workflow instance. Combined with wosdefid to form an exact version reference for audit and replay.

wosstate.description: The current lifecycle state of the workflow instance at the time of event emission. Helps consumers filter events by workflow phase without fetching full instance state.

wostaskid.description: The identifier of the task that triggered this event, if applicable. Correlates the event to a specific task within the workflow's task list for traceability.

woscorrelationkey.description: The primary business correlation key for this workflow instance. A stable business identifier (e.g., application ID, case number) that persists across retries and replays for operational correlation.

woscausationeventid.description: The CloudEvents id attribute of the event that causally triggered this event. Enables construction of causal chains for audit trails and replay analysis.

ExtensionsMap.description: Vendor extension data attached to this node. All keys MUST start with 'x-' (see Kernel §10.6). The reserved namespace 'x-wos-*' is for WOS Working Group use only; third-party extensions MUST use a unique vendor prefix (e.g., 'x-acme-', 'x-vendor-'). Processors MUST ignore unknown extension keys to preserve forward compatibility. Extension values are unconstrained — any JSON value is valid, but authors are encouraged to document the shape in their vendor spec.

JsonSchemaUri.description: Optional JSON Schema URI enabling editor validation and autocompletion. When present, editors (VS Code, IntelliJ, etc.) validate the document against this schema. Omit in production documents; the authoritative schema URI is derived from the document type marker (e.g., '$wosKernel': '1.0'). Used for author-time tooling only — runtime processors MUST ignore this field.
